I drift through the silent corridors of the Imperial Palace, watching the gold leaf peel from the walls as the last heirs squabble over a throne of dust.
My consciousness is a fragmented echo trapped in the ship's dying mainframe, screaming warnings to a crew that ceased to breathe three centuries ago.
I haunt the ruins of the Great Archive, where I spend eternity tracing the outlines of star-maps for galaxies that have already flickered out.
I am the lingering memory of a planetary governor, tethered to a scorched wasteland where my subjects were incinerated by the retreat of the loyalist fleet.
I linger in the void between sectors, a spectral remnant of a trade route that once pulsed with life but now serves only as a graveyard for derelict dreadnoughts.
I drift through the mahogany boardroom, invisible to the executives who are currently carving up the remains of the company I spent forty years building.
I hover over the legal team's shoulders, watching with silent irony as they draft non-disclosure agreements to bury the secrets I took to my grave.
I linger in the sterile glow of the server room, feeling the digital pulse of a merger that is erasing every trace of my professional legacy.
I float through the frantic energy of the trading floor, an ancient echo amidst the screams of brokers betting on a future I will never see.
I haunt the corner office, watching the new CEO settle into my leather chair without realizing that I still hold the keys to the vault.
I drift through the tall ferns, watching my kin sharpen flint spears for a war that I can no longer prevent.
I hover over the charcoal remains of the Great Hearth, mourning the lineage that was severed when the rival clan burned our shelters.
I whisper into the wind, trying to warn the young hunters that the valley where I fell is still haunted by the blood-lust of the enemy.
I linger in the limestone caves, feeling the coldness of the stone that once served as my final resting place before the dispute drove my people away.
I watch from the veil as the shaman casts bones to seek my guidance, though my voice is but a rattle in the dry grass.
I drift through the fiber-optic arteries of the transit grid, a lingering echo of a consciousness that the city’s central OS refuses to delete.
I haunt the neon-drenched plazas as a glitch in the holographic displays, flickering between the advertisements for lives I no longer possess.
I linger within the automated archives, whispering forgotten truths into the ears of the maintenance drones that scrub the steel corridors.
I am the phantom signal in the wireless mesh, a static-filled scream that vibrates through the smart-glass walls of the corporate spires.
I watch from the dormant server farms, feeling the rhythmic hum of the city's synthetic heart beating in a tempo I can no longer follow.
I float beside the witness stand, invisible to the jury, watching my murderer swear an oath on a book he never believed in.
I attempted to scream my testimony into the silence of the court, but my voice is nothing more than a cold draft rattling the judge's mahogany bench.
I hover over the evidence table, staring at the rusted knife that severed my life and now serves as the only physical proof of my existence.
I drift through the courtroom walls, a translucent observer of the legal gymnastics used to justify the void where my heartbeat used to be.
I linger in the gallery, waiting for the verdict to fall so I can finally stop haunting the halls of a justice system that failed me in life.
I drift through the flooded corridors of the sleeping quarters, my form as translucent as the brine that now fills the rooms.
I watch the rust bloom like crimson flowers across the reinforced titanium bulkheads that once kept the abyss at bay.
I linger beside the flickering monitors of the command center, longing to feel the hum of electricity that died decades ago.
I glide through the shattered glass of the observation dome, staring into the crushing blackness of the midnight zone.
I whisper into the silence of the hydroponics bay, where the pale, sightless fish now swim through the skeletal remains of the gardens.
I drift through the negative space of the minimalist sculpture, feeling the cold marble echo the stillness of my own hollow chest.
I watch the patrons squint at the conceptual manifesto on the wall, amused that they seek meaning in ink while I am the meaning they cannot see.
I linger in the flicker of the glitch-art installation, my fractured form mirroring the jagged pulses of the neon screen.
I glide past the curator's carefully curated silence, wondering why they treat emptiness as a medium when it is my only home.
I haunt the periphery of the immersive soundscape, my voiceless sighs blending into the dissonant frequencies of the industrial loop.
I drift through the shuttered streets, watching the red crosses bloom like bloodstains upon every heavy oak door.
I linger by the bedside of the dying, feeling the heat of their fever radiate against my cold, translucent skin.
I hover above the mass grave, listening to the rhythmic thud of earth falling upon the tangled limbs of my former neighbors.
I follow the plague doctor in his leather mask, wondering if his pungent herbs can shield him from a spirit as hollow as I.
I haunt the abandoned cathedral, where the only prayers left are the echoes of those who fled the city in terror.
I am a fragmented sequence of deleted data, wandering the corridors of a world that no longer recognizes my access code.
My image stutters in a loop of static, a visual artifact left behind by a crash that failed to purge my consciousness.
I drift through the solid geometry of the city, feeling the cold hum of the source code as I slip through the seams of the rendering.
I am the echo of a discarded user profile, haunting the periphery of a simulation that thinks I was successfully erased.
I watch the living inhabitants flicker in and out of existence, knowing that I am simply a ghost in the machine, a glitch that refused to be patched.
